# Prerequisites {.unnumbered}

Just like with anything we have to walk before we can run.

## Learning the Linux CLI

A thorough treatment of Linux CLI fundamentals geared towards the novice is
outside of the scope of this book. But if you're a beginner I can thoroughly
endorse any of the following resources.

1. [Learning the Shell](https://www.linuxcommand.org/lc3_learning_the_shell.php)
  from **LinuxCommand.org**

::: {.callout-tip}
While you're there I also recommend checking out 
[The Linux Command Line](https://www.linuxcommand.org/tlcl.php) by William
Shotts. This is my #1 go-to recommendation for beginners that want to learn
Linux and the Linux CLI. This is where I first learned Linux and the Linux CLI
many years ago.
:::

2. [Command Line Crash Course](https://learnpythonthehardway.org/book/appendixa.html)
  from **Learn Python the Hard Way** by Zed Shaw
3. MIT **Missing Semester** Series:
  [Introduction to the Shell](https://missing.csail.mit.edu/2026/course-shell/) &
  [Command-line Environment](https://missing.csail.mit.edu/2026/command-line-environment/)

Other notable mentions:

4. [Linux Upskill Challenge](https://linuxupskillchallenge.org/)\
  This is a shameless plug, as I'm involved with this project.
5. [Linux Commands for Beginners](https://youtube.com/playlist?list=PLT98CRl2KxKHaKA9-4_I38sLzK134p4GJ&si=fhFuZX3oHMk0puAO)
  YouTube Playlist by [@LearnLinuxTV](https://www.youtube.com/@LearnLinuxTV)

Becoming proficient in the Linux CLI is a pre-requisite to learning RHEL and
preparing for the RHCSA exam. It's worth spending some time on acquiring this
skill if you can afford it.
